documents.title: Implementing Australia's AI Ethics Principles: A selection of Responsible AI practices and resources
documents.publisher: CSIRO National AI Centre (prepared by Gradient Institute)
documents.what_it_is: Implementing Australia's AI Ethics Principles: A selection of Responsible AI practices and resources

structure.section:
1.1 
The need for Responsible 
AI in business

Account for the needs 
of all stakeholders while 
promoting inclusivity 
and equity.

Ensure that AI systems 
are explicitly designed to 
operate ethically (as well 
as meeting their business 
objectives).

Throughout their lifecycle, AI systems should benefit individuals, society and 
the environment.

Respect and preserve 
human agency and 
decision-making 
capabilities.

Justify the means by 
which outcomes are 
achieved.

Throughout their lifecycle, AI systems should respect human rights, diversity, 
and the autonomy of individuals.
Resources for diversity and inclusion in AI

Consider what is fair 
in the context of the 
system’s impacts and 
who is affected.

Quantify fairness 
concerns to promote 
effective oversight and 
mitigation.

Reduce the risk of the 
system introducing, 
perpetuating or 
amplifying societal 
inequalities.

Ensure 
established legal 
and technical 
practices are 
applied.

Ensure that 
malicious 
actors cannot 
manipulate or 
compromise 
the system or 
its data.

Only use the 
most relevant 
data records 
and attributes 
to reduce risk of 
privacy exposure.

Use algorithms 
that minimise 
privacy exposure 
by design.

5.3 Minimise the collection 
of personal information

Ensure high quality data 
to promote high quality 
model outputs.

Test the assumptions of 
the system at a limited 
scale to reduce exposure 
to unforeseen impacts.

Oversee performance 
against both business 
and ethical objectives 
to ensure the system is 
operating as intended.

Throughout their lifecycle, AI systems should reliably operate in accordance with 
their intended purpose.

Inform users of an AI’s 
operation to build trust 
and empower them to 
make effective decisions.

Promote deliberate 
reflection within the 
development team 
and incentivise rigour 
through external scrutiny.

Help stakeholders 
understand the system’s 
decision process to 
build trust and uncover 
problems.

Determine proportionate 
means for affected 
stakeholders to voice 
their objections.

Allow human decision 
makers to provide 
context and compassion 
that the system may lack.

Provide the means for 
an impacted individual 
to mount a successful 
contest.

8.2 Establish human review 
of contested decisions

Ensure that 
individuals are 
equipped to 
make ethical 
decisions when 
designing and 
deploying AI 
systems.

Be clear 
about who is 
accountable for 
different aspects 
of the AI system’s 
operation and 
impacts.

Drive and 
reinforce 
responsible 
AI practices 
by explicitly 
motivating 
ethical behaviour.

9.1 
Raise awareness and 
knowledge of Responsible AI

The National AI Centre is 
building Australia’s responsible 
and inclusive AI future.

documents.publisher: Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet
documents.what_it_is: How might artificial intelligence affect the trustworthiness of public service delivery?

structure.section:
How might artificial intelligence 
affect the trustworthiness of 
public service delivery?

Artificial intelligence offers significant opportunities to improve 
public service delivery
Stewardship of artificial intelligence in public service delivery
Framework for trustworthy use of AI in public service delivery
The state of trust in the public service’s use of AI

Artificial intelligence and the trustworthiness of 
public service delivery

Insight 1: Artificial Intelligence must be designed and 
implemented with integrity

Insight 2: Using artificial intelligence shouldn’t come at the 
expense of empathy

Insight 3: Artificial intelligence should improve the performance 
of public services

Insight 4: Successful service delivery depends on supporting 
people to engage with AI-enabled services in the long term
